source_text: Handshake messages MUST NOT be interleaved with other record types. That is, if a handshake message is split over two or more records, there MUST NOT be any other records between them.
rule_statement: Handshake messages MUST NOT be interleaved with other record types; no other records between fragments of a handshake message.
applies_when: Handshake message is fragmented across multiple records
expected_value: no non-handshake records between handshake fragments
checkability_rationale: Can verify no interleaved records during handshake fragmentation.

source_text: Handshake messages MUST NOT span key changes. Implementations MUST verify that all messages immediately preceding a key change align with a record boundary; if not, then they MUST terminate the connection with an "unexpected_message" alert.
rule_statement: Handshake messages MUST NOT span key changes. Messages preceding a key change MUST align with record boundary or terminate with unexpected_message.
expected_value: messages before key change end at record boundary
checkability_rationale: Can verify record boundary alignment at key transitions.
extraction_confidence_rationale: Explicit MUST NOT and MUST with specified alert.

source_text: Because the ClientHello, EndOfEarlyData, ServerHello, Finished, and KeyUpdate messages can immediately precede a key change, implementations MUST send these messages in alignment with a record boundary.
rule_statement: ClientHello, EndOfEarlyData, ServerHello, Finished, and KeyUpdate MUST be sent aligned with a record boundary.
applies_when: Sending messages that can precede key changes
checkability_rationale: Can verify these messages are not coalesced with subsequent messages in same record.

source_text: Implementations MUST NOT send zero-length fragments of Handshake types, even if those fragments contain padding.
rule_statement: Implementations MUST NOT send zero-length fragments of Handshake type records.
checkability_rationale: Can verify no zero-length handshake records are sent.

source_text: Alert messages (Section 6) MUST NOT be fragmented across records, and multiple alert messages MUST NOT be coalesced into a single TLSPlaintext record. In other words, a record with an Alert type MUST contain exactly one message.
rule_statement: Alert records MUST contain exactly one alert message — no fragmentation and no coalescing.
checkability_rationale: Can verify alert record length equals exactly 2 bytes.

source_text: legacy_record_version: MUST be set to 0x0303 for all records generated by a TLS 1.3 implementation other than an initial ClientHello (i.e., one not generated after a HelloRetryRequest), where it MAY also be 0x0301 for compatibility purposes.
rule_statement: legacy_record_version MUST be 0x0303 for all TLS 1.3 records except initial ClientHello (which MAY be 0x0301).
checkability_rationale: Direct value check on record header field.

source_text: This field is deprecated and MUST be ignored for all purposes.
rule_statement: Receivers MUST ignore the legacy_record_version field for all purposes.
checkability_rationale: Can verify receiver accepts records with non-standard version field values.

source_text: The length MUST NOT exceed 2^14 bytes. An endpoint that receives a record that exceeds this length MUST terminate the connection with a "record_overflow" alert.
rule_statement: TLSPlaintext.length MUST NOT exceed 2^14 bytes; violation triggers record_overflow alert.
checkability_rationale: Direct value range check on length field.
extraction_confidence_rationale: Explicit MUST NOT with specified alert.

source_text: In order to maximize backward compatibility, a record containing an initial ClientHello SHOULD have version 0x0301 (reflecting TLS 1.0) and a record containing a second ClientHello or a ServerHello MUST have version 0x0303 (reflecting TLS 1.2).
rule_statement: Initial ClientHello record SHOULD have legacy_record_version 0x0301; second ClientHello or ServerHello record MUST have 0x0303.
applies_when: Sending initial ClientHello, second ClientHello, or ServerHello record
expected_value: 0x0301 for initial CH (SHOULD), 0x0303 for second CH or SH (MUST)
checkability_rationale: Direct value check on record version field.

source_text: Note that Application Data records MUST NOT be written to the wire unprotected (see Section 2 for details).
rule_statement: Application Data records MUST NOT be sent unprotected (before record protection is engaged).
checkability_rationale: Can verify no plaintext application data records are sent.
